entry.title: Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey Returns for 2025 Thanksgiving Season
entry.summary:
Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey makes its annual return for the 2025 Thanksgiving season.
Available for a limited time at participating locations as well as online for nationwide (continental US only) shipping to your door, the pre-seasoned, fully-cooked turkey needs to be thawed and reheated before eating. Each Cajun-Style Turkey weighs about 11 to 13 pounds and is meant to serve eight to 12 people. You can pre-order it in person or through the Popeyes website or mobile app. Pricing for the Cajun turkey starts at $54.99 when purchased from a Popeyes restaurant and $99.99 to get one shipped to your home. If you're curious as to how Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey turns out, I tried and reviewed one a while back. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Debuts New Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito
entry.summary:
Taco Bell brings back the flavor of Flamin' Hot with the launch of its new Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito, which features both Flamin' Hot Fritos corn chips and new Flamin' Hot Rice.
In addition to Flamin' Hot Fritos and Flamin' Hot Rice, the Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito also contains seasoned beef, a three-cheese blend, Creamy Chipotle sauce, warm nacho cheese sauce, and reduced-fat sour cream inside a warm flour tortilla topped with grilled cheese. The limited-time item has its roots in the Beefy Crunch Burrito but is described as "cheesier" and "spicier." Taco Bell's new Flamin' Hot Rice is made with Fritos Flamin' Hot Chips, red peppers, cheddar cheese powder, garlic, paprika, and lime. In addition to being found in the new burrito, you can also purchase it as a side for $1. The Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito is available at participating locations nationwide. You can buy one a la carte for a suggested price of $5.49, while supplies last. It's also showcased in a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box that includes the burrito, a Chicken Chalupa Supreme, a Crunchy Taco, Chips & Nacho Cheese Sauce, and a medium fountain drink for $9. Photo via Taco Bell. Nutritional Info - Taco Bell Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito Calories - 730 Fat - 41g (Saturated Fat - 17g) Sodium - 1270mg Carbs - 65g (Sugar - 4g) Protein - 26g Read more at Brand Eating!

Cafe-bakery chain Paris Baguette welcomes a slate of Halloween-themed cakes and donuts for the spooky holiday. This year's Halloween lineup includes:
- Dirt & Worms Chocolate Chiffon Cake - Riffing on dirt cups, it features chocolate chiffon cake layered with cookies & cream soft cream with cookie "dirt" and gummy worms. - Dirt & Worms King Cream Donut - A soft donut filled with cookies & cream custard, topped with cookies & cream white chocolate, cookie "dirt," and a gummy worm. - Vanilla Scream Ghost Cake - Vanilla sponge cake layered with custard cream, decorated with soft cream ghosts and chocolate spider webs. - Spider Mochi Donut - A mochi donut covered in cookies & cream white chocolate, topped with a chocolate sandwich cookie and drizzled with chocolate "spider legs." As part of the Halloween festivities, Paris Baguette will also be offering a free pastry with any beverage purchase to its rewards members on Halloween (October 31, 2025). Photo via Paris Baguette. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Cheez-It Flavored Taco Shells Arrive
entry.summary:
Mexican food brand Ortega partners with Cheez-It to launch new Cheez-It Flavored Taco Shells.
The crunchy hard taco shell is infused with the cheesy flavor of Cheez-It crackers. In addition to being filled to make tacos, the shell is also meant to be enjoyed as a snack right out of the box. You can find new Ortega Cheez-It Flavored Taco Shells at Target (where a 4.6-oz box containing 10 taco shells costs $3.19 near me; the price varies with location) and select retailers nationwide. Availability at additional grocery stores is slated for the coming months. Photo via Ortega. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Krispy Kreme's 2025 Halloween Donuts Arrive
entry.summary:
Krispy Kreme's 2025 Halloween donuts are here for a limited time as the "Trick or Treat! Collection." Featuring four new, festive donuts (in a Halloween-themed box if you buy a dozen), here's what you can expect:
- The Candy Bag Donut - An Original Glazed donut topped with chocolate-flavored buttercreme, a buttery caramel-flavored swirl, and a sprinkle of trick-or-treat candy blend. - Cookies and Skreme House Donut - A shell donut filled with Cookies & Kreme filling, dipped in chocolate icing, and topped with crunchy silver chocolate cookies, a wavy line of purple buttercream-flavored icing, and a spooky Haunted House piece. - Jack-O-Lantern Donut - A shell donut topped with orange buttercream-flavored icing, Jack-O-Lantern face pieces, and a green icing stem. - Spooky Sprinkle Donut - An Original Glazed donut topped with green icing and Halloween sprinkles. As part of this year's Halloween festivities, Krispy Kreme will be giving out a free Original Glazed or Classic Ring donut for costumed visitors at participating shops on Saturday, October 25, 2025, and again on Friday, October 31, 2025 (no purchase necessary). Krispy Kreme's Trick or Treat! Collection is available now at participating locations nationwide. They can also be found in a 6-pack delivered daily to select retailers. Photo via Krispy Kreme. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Reveals New Frank's RedHot Diablo Sauce Menu
entry.summary:
Taco Bell unveils its new Frank's RedHot Diablo menu, which features a mix of "original Buffalo flavor, Taco Bell’s signature Diablo sauce, and spicy ranch." With the launch of the menu on October 16, 2025, Crispy Chicken Strips and Nacho Fries are back for a limited time.
Here's what you can expect to find on the new, limited-time menu: - Frank's RedHot Diablo Crispy Chicken Burrito - Crispy chicken strips, shredded lettuce, purple cabbage, pico de gallo, cheddar cheese, and a choice of Frank's RedHot Diablo or Avocado Ranch, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla and then grilled. The suggested price for the burrito is $5.49. - Frank's RedHot Diablo Crispy Chicken Soft Taco - A Crispy Chicken strip, purple cabbage, lettuce, pico de gallo, cheddar cheese, and a choice of Frank's RedHot Diablo or Avocado Ranch, served folded in a soft flour tortilla. It carries a suggested price of $2.99. - Frank's RedHot Diablo Chicken Nacho Fries - Seasoned Nacho Fries topped with slow-roasted chicken, nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend, pico de gallo, and a drizzle of Frank's RedHot Diablo. The suggested price for this one is $4.99. - Crispy Chicken Strips with Spicy Ranch - Four crispy-fried strips of white meat chicken coated in a crunchy tortilla chip breading and served with a choice of Spicy Ranch or Frank’s RedHot® Diablo sauce. The suggested price tag for an order comes out to $6.99. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Apple Crisp Returns to Starbucks
entry.summary:
Apple Crisp (AKA apple brown sugar syrup) is back for a "very limited time" at Starbucks in the US, while supplies last.
The fall flavor is highlighted in the Iced Apple Crisp Oatmilk Shaken Espresso. The drink features Starbucks Blonde Espresso shaken with apple brown sugar syrup, poured over ice, and topped with oat milk. While it's available, you can also add apple brown sugar syrup to any beverage. It's also used to make Apple Crispy Cold Foam and new Apple Crispy Protein Cold Foam. Both can be added to any iced drink as a customization for an extra charge. Photo via Starbucks. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Sonic Launches New Bourbon Caramel Iced Coffee
entry.summary:
Sonic adds to its iced coffee options with the launch of new Bourbon Caramel Iced Coffee. The new flavor is available starting October 20, 2025, as a Sonic app-exclusive flavor ahead of a national launch on November 3, 2025.
The flavor features cold brew iced coffee with bourbon caramel flavor (non-alcoholic), crowned with whipped topping, a caramel drizzle, and a mini cowboy hat garnish. Bourbon Caramel Iced Coffee was inspired by the winning creation from the TV series "On Brand with Jimmy Fallon." The show challenges creative contestants to design campaigns for various brands. The average price for the new coffee flavor is $3.69 for a medium. Photo via Sonic Drive-in. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Sweet Chili Chicken Strips Arrive at Dairy Queen
entry.summary:
Dairy Queen launches new Sweet Chili Chicken Strips as a new flavor for its Sauced & Tossed Chicken Strips Baskets.
The Sweet Chili sauce is a limited-time option that the chain describes as a "combination of tangy sweetness and a subtle kick of heat." Sweet chili sauce is a Thai sauce made with red chili peppers that is sweet, spicy, and slightly tangy. The Sweet Chili Sauced & Tossed Chicken Strips Basket features four or six chicken strips tossed in new Sweet Chili sauce, served with fries, Texas toast, and a choice of dipping sauce. Dairy Queen also offers Honey BBQ as a Sauced & Tossed flavor for its chicken strips. Photo via Dairy Queen. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Starbucks Welcomes New Protein Cold Foam and Protein Lattes
entry.summary:
Starbucks introduces new Protein Cold Foam drinks and Protein Lattes, featuring up to 36 grams of protein in a grande-sized beverage. The extra protein comes from whey protein isolate.
Starbucks Protein Cold Foam (pictured) can be added to any cold coffee, tea, or Refreshers, and adds 15 grams of protein to a grande. It costs $2 extra (prices may vary) and comes in several flavors, including new banana, vanilla, chocolate, matcha, salted caramel brown sugar, plain, sugar-free vanilla, pumpkin (seasonal), and pecan (seasonal). Starbucks Protein-Boosted Milk, made by blending 2% milk with whey protein isolate, can be substituted for regular milk in any drink that contains milk for $1 more (prices may vary). In addition, the coffeehouse chain adds several Protein Cold Foam beverages to the menu, including the new Iced Banana Cream Protein Matcha, Iced Vanilla Cream Protein Latte, and Chocolate Cream Protein Cold Brew. They're joined by new Protein Lattes, including new Sugar-Free Vanilla Protein Matcha, Sugar-Free Vanilla Protein Latte, Protein Matcha, and Vanilla Protein Matcha. The lattes are available iced or hot, while the Cold Foam drinks are only available iced. Both Protein Cold Foam and Protein Lattes are available now in the U.S. and Canada. Photo via Starbucks. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Chick-fil-A Releases New Waffle Potato Chips in Grocery Stores
entry.summary:
Chick-fil-A expands its retail lineup with the test launch of new Waffle Potato Chips at select grocery stores in 12 states in the Southeast and Atlantic regions starting this month.
The release follows the restaurant debut of Waffle Potato Chips earlier this year. Chick-fil-A Waffle Potato Chips come in two varieties: Chick-fil-A Sauce Flavored Waffle Potato Chips and Original Flavor Waffle Potato Chips. The former offers the smoky, tangy, and slightly sweet taste of Chick-fil-A's signature sauce, while the latter features classic waffle-cut potato chips seasoned with sea salt. Both varieties are available now in 7-oz bags at select Publix, Kroger, and Walmart stores in Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia, Kentucky, Ohio, Arkansas, and Missouri. The chips join a Chick-fil-A retail lineup that includes sauces and dressings. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!
